# DDx Story 12 — Optimize the prompts used to operate `ddx run` (final)

## Scope clarification

`ddx run` is a thin pass-through (`service_run.go:114`, `run_internal.go`) —
no DDx-owned system prompt. The static prompts DDx ships on every attempt and
that the user actually means to optimize are:

1. `executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` — execute_bead.go:1208 — ~1078 words / ~1400 tok
2. `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`  — execute_bead.go:1274 — ~1020 words / ~1330 tok
3. `beadReviewInstructions`            — execute_bead_review.go:124 — ~283 words / ~370 tok (LEAVE)
4. `executeBeadMissingGoverningText`   — execute_bead.go:1357 — ~42 words (LEAVE)

Selector at execute_bead.go:1343 routes `agent`/`fiz`/`embedded` to the Agent
variant; everything else (`claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, unknown) uses the
Claude variant. This grouping is intentional — see plan-2026-04-18 §112 and
the portable-prompt principle (frame/principles.md:152). Story 12 keeps the
two-variant split.

## Diagnosis

### Load-bearing guardrails (every one MUST survive any rewrite)

Codex audit caught the original list missing several. Final list:

- AC checkbox is satisfied by a specific code/test/file (anti-handwave) — execute_bead.go:1208,1234
- Read first / read named files / read referenced specs before editing — execute_bead.go:1232,1300
- Missing-governing fallback note — execute_bead.go:1357
- Commit subject ends with `[<bead-id>]` — execute_bead.go:1238,1308
- Commit exactly once, when green — execute_bead.go:1238,1308
- `git add <specific-paths>`, never `git add -A` — execute_bead.go:1238,1308
- Do not commit red code — execute_bead.go:1236,1306
- Do not modify files outside bead scope — execute_bead.go:1261
- Never run `ddx init` — execute_bead.go:1260,1334
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — execute_bead.go:1259,1333
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md unless asked — execute_bead.go:1262,1335
- Bead description overrides CLAUDE.md / YAGNI defaults — execute_bead.go:1248,1328
- Reports go under `{{.AttemptDir}}/`, never `/tmp`, and are committed alongside code — execute_bead.go:1244,1318
- Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` before exiting empty — execute_bead.go:1240,1314
- Step 0 size-check + decomposition path — execute_bead.go:1210,1278
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do NOT declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open — execute_bead.go:1254,1324 (Agent variant has stronger phrasing)
- Stop after the commit succeeds (Agent post-commit runaway guard) — execute_bead.go:1310
- Agent-only: use tool calls, not `bash: cat`/`rg`/`ls` — execute_bead.go:1304
- Reviewer JSON fence + anti-echo rules — execute_bead_review.go:132–151

### Redundancy across the two execute-bead variants

Step 0 size check, "investigation reports under {{.AttemptDir}}/", "review
gate / `<review-findings>`", "bead overrides project defaults", and most of
the constraints tail are duplicated almost verbatim. Source-level dedup will
remove ~250 words of repeated text. Per-attempt dedup (after tightening)
targets ~30%+ word reduction in the rendered prompt.

### Note on prompt caching (codex catch)

`buildPrompt` at execute_bead.go:1392–1461 places dynamic bead metadata FIRST
and static instructions LAST. That means Anthropic's prefix-cache cannot help
at all today — every attempt cache-misses on the static suffix. Reordering
to put static instructions first would unlock cache hits across attempts
with the same prompt version. **Capture this as a follow-up bead** (B5) but
do not block Story 12 on it; reordering changes XML semantics and merits
its own change with separate review.

## Spec changes

### FEAT-022 amendment

Add under Functional / Shared evidence primitives:

> **Static-prompt minimum-prompt rule.** Every static instruction string DDx
> embeds in an LLM prompt MUST declare its load-bearing guardrails inline
> as a Go comment list above the constant, one bullet per guardrail with a
> pointer to the failure mode it prevents. Edits MUST preserve every listed
> guardrail. New guardrails MUST be added to the list. A regression test
> MUST assert each listed guardrail still appears in the rendered prompt.
> This rule applies to STATIC prompt bodies; dynamic evidence egress is
> covered by the §5–§7 cap primitives and is unaffected.

The closing sentence is per codex's correction: do not let this amendment
imply static-comment guardrails replace the existing dynamic-egress caps.

### New TD: prompt versioning + offline comparison + rendered-size report

Captures the measurement roadmap. Summary:

- Add `prompt_sha` (and later `prompt_version`) to `manifest.json` so
  attempt outcomes slice cleanly across rewrites using existing telemetry
  (usage.json, session_index.go session tokens, resolver_feat008.go
  efficacy view).
- Add a deterministic test fixture that renders both variants for a
  representative bead and reports byte / word counts, gated as a
  CI artifact (catches accidental bloat).
- Optional: `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT=<name>` selector for offline A/B.

## Implementation plan

1. **B1 — Extract shared blocks (refactor only).**
   - Pull duplicated text into named constants:
     `instrStep0SizeCheck`, `instrInvestigationReports`,
     `instrReviewGate`, `instrBeadOverride`, `instrCoreConstraints`.
   - Both variants compose: per-harness preamble + per-harness process body
     + shared blocks.
   - Rendered prompt MUST be byte-identical (or trivially equivalent —
     whitespace only) to the current output. All existing tests pass
     unchanged.

2. **B2 — Tighten shared blocks for density.**
   - Step 0: prose → 4-bullet checklist + 4-step recipe (~120 → ~80 w).
   - Process: drop verification-command examples (the bead names them);
     keep "do not commit red code" as one bullet; keep `[<bead-id>]`,
     `git add <specific-paths>`, "commit exactly once", "stop after
     commit", "do not modify outside bead scope" as separate bullets
     (codex catch — must remain explicit). (~150 → ~85 w)
   - Review-gate: two bullets ("review is a gate"; "address every BLOCKING
     `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking
     findings open"). (~80 → ~45 w)
   - Bead-override: one sentence. (~70 → ~25 w)
   - Constraints tail: bullets, including `.ddx/executions/` intact and
     "never `ddx init`" as separate items. (~60 → ~40 w)
   - Add the load-bearing-guardrail comment block above each constant per
     FEAT-022 amendment.

3. **B3 — Render-size report + `prompt_sha` in manifest (lightweight v1
   measurement).**
   - Test fixture renders representative-bead prompt for each
     (variant, contextBudget) and prints word / byte count to a CI artifact.
   - `manifest.json` gains `prompt_sha` (sha256 of the rendered prompt).
     Existing analytics in resolver_feat008.go can group attempts by
     prompt_sha for before/after comparison. Codex insisted on shipping
     measurement in v1, not deferring entirely.

4. **B4 — Amend FEAT-022 + author TD.**
   - FEAT-022 minimum-prompt rule paragraph.
   - New TD captures `prompt_version`, `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` selector, and
     the offline-comparison roadmap (NOT implemented in this story).

5. **B5 — (deferred / optional) Reorder buildPrompt to put static
   instructions first for prefix-cache compatibility.** Not on Story 12
   critical path; file the bead, do not work it here.

## Acceptance criteria

- AC1: After B1, both variants share ≥4 named instruction-block constants;
  no shared block text is duplicated across variants in source.
- AC2: After B2, rendered prompt for the representative bead is ≥30% shorter
  (word count) than current for both Claude and Agent variants.
- AC3: New test `TestExecuteBeadInstructionsLoadBearingGuardrails` asserts
  every guardrail listed in the file-level comment block (final list above)
  appears in the rendered prompt for each (harness, variant). Includes
  selector tests: `agent`/`fiz`/`embedded` → Agent; `claude`/`codex`/
  `opencode`/unknown → Claude (per codex catch).
- AC4: New invariant tests assert: rendered prompts contain
  `[<bead-id>]`, `git add <specific-paths>`, no `git add -A`,
  `no_changes_rationale.txt`, `.ddx/executions/`, `ddx bead create`,
  `ddx bead dep add`, `ddx bead update` (per codex catch).
- AC5: New test asserts non-minimal prompts include
  `executeBeadMissingGoverningText`; `contextBudget == "minimal"` omits
  governing refs as today.
- AC6: All existing prompt tests pass unchanged
  (`execute_bead_evidence_path_test.go`, `review_verdict_test.go`,
  `execute_bead_failure_mode_test.go`).
- AC7: `manifest.json` contains `prompt_sha` for every attempt; size-report
  fixture emits word/byte counts per (variant, contextBudget).
- AC8: FEAT-022 amended; new TD authored.
- AC9: `beadReviewInstructions` is unchanged. (NOT "out of scope" — the
  schema/fence guardrails are load-bearing and dynamic-evidence cost is
  already addressed by `BuildReviewPromptBounded`. Per codex.)
- AC10: No new per-harness branches beyond Claude/Agent.

## Tests (consolidated)

- Existing: `go test ./cli/internal/agent/...` passes unchanged.
- New: AC3 guardrail-list test.
- New: AC3 selector test.
- New: AC4 substring-invariant tests.
- New: AC5 missing-governing test.
- New: AC2 size-floor test (word count threshold per variant).
- New: AC9 reviewer-prompt regression test (fenced-JSON requirement, no
  prose, no template echo) — explicit re-assertion.

## Risks (post-codex)

- Guardrail loss — mitigated by FEAT-022 comment-list contract + AC3 test.
- Story 10 collision on Step 0 — serialize. (See coordination.)
- Per-harness drift — shared blocks; per-variant only owns preamble +
  process body.
- Cheap-tier behavior change on shorter prompts — `prompt_sha` in manifest
  + size-report fixture (B3) lets us slice efficacy/no_evidence_produced
  across the rewrite. If the metric worsens, revert is one constant edit.
- FEAT-022 amendment phrasing must NOT imply static-comment guardrails
  replace dynamic-egress caps (codex catch).
- `beadReviewInstructions` left alone is correct; reviewer's real cost is
  diff inlining, already bounded by `BuildReviewPromptBounded`.
- Prompt caching unaddressed in Story 12; B5 captured separately.

## Bead breakdown (final, with codex's split on B4)

- **B1 — Extract shared instruction blocks (refactor, byte-equivalent
  output).** Tier: cheap. Deps: Story 10 prompt edits merged.
  AC: AC1, AC6.
- **B2 — Tighten shared blocks; add guardrail-list comment per FEAT-022;
  add invariant + selector + missing-governing tests.** Tier: cheap;
  review at smart. Deps: B1.
  AC: AC2, AC3, AC4, AC5, AC6, AC10.
- **B3 — Add `prompt_sha` to manifest.json + render-size CI fixture.**
  Tier: cheap. Deps: none (can run in parallel with B1/B2 — treats prompt
  as opaque).
  AC: AC7.
- **B4a — FEAT-022 amendment (minimum-prompt rule).** Tier: cheap. Deps:
  B2 (so the rule reflects what was done).
  AC: AC8 (FEAT-022 portion).
- **B4b — TD for prompt versioning, `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` selector,
  offline comparison.** Tier: cheap. Deps: B4a.
  AC: AC8 (TD portion).
- **B5 (optional, not part of Story 12 critical path) — Reorder
  `buildPrompt` to place static instructions before bead metadata for
  prefix-cache compatibility.** Tier: standard. Deps: B2. File now,
  prioritize separately.

## Coordination with Story 10

Story 10 (automatic escalation + always-review) has not yet returned a plan
file (`/tmp/story-10-final.md` does not exist as of this write). Story 10
edits the SAME Step 0 / decomposition section that B1+B2 dedup and tighten.
**Hard recommendation:** do not start B1 or B2 until Story 10's prompt
edits land; if both stories drain in the same run, file B1 with a hard
`dep` on Story 10's terminal bead. **Parallelizable carve-outs (codex):**
B3 (prompt_sha + size fixture) and B4b (TD authoring, if it avoids
quoting Step 0 final wording) can run in parallel with Story 10. B4a
should wait until B2 is done.

1352	// governing references were pre-resolved for the bead. The bead description

1358	func xmlEscape(s string) string {
1359		var buf bytes.Buffer
1360		_ = xml.EscapeText(&buf, []byte(s))
1361		return buf.String()
1362	}

1364	func xmlAttrEscape(s string) string {
1365		r := strings.NewReplacer(
1366			"&", "&",
1367			"<", "<",
1368			">", ">",
1369			"\"", """,
1370			"'", "'",
1371			"\n", "
",
1372			"\r", "",
1373			"\t", "	",
1374		)
1375		return r.Replace(s)
1376	}

1378	func buildPrompt(workDir string, b *bead.Bead, refs []executeBeadGoverningRef, artifacts *executeBeadArtifacts, baseRev, promptOverride, harness string, contextBudget string) ([]byte, string, error) {
1379		if strings.TrimSpace(promptOverride) != "" {
1380			path := promptOverride
1381			if !filepath.IsAbs(path) {
1382				path = filepath.Join(workDir, path)
1383			}
1384			data, err := readPromptFileBounded(path)
1385			if err != nil {
1386				return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("reading prompt override %q: %w", promptOverride, err)
1387			}
1388			return data, promptOverride, nil
1389		}
1390	
1391		var sb strings.Builder
1392		sb.WriteString("<execute-bead>\n")
1393	
1394		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=\"%s\">\n", xmlAttrEscape(b.ID))
1395		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", xmlEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
1396	
1397		desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description)
1398		if desc == "" {
1399			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
1400		} else {
1401			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", xmlEscape(desc))
1402		}
1403	
1404		acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance)
1405		if acc == "" {
1406			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
1407		} else {
1408			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", xmlEscape(acc))
1409		}
1410	
1411		// Bead notes carry review findings from prior iterations, escalation
1412		// context, or operator hints that did not fit into the description at
1413		// creation time. Threading them into the prompt as a distinct section
1414		// lets the agent act on them without the operator having to rewrite the
1415		// description in place on every reopen.
1416		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
1417			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", xmlEscape(notes))
1418		}
1419	
1420		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
1421			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", xmlEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
1422		} else {
1423			sb.WriteString("    <labels/>\n")
1424		}
1425	
1426		metaAttrs := make([]string, 0, 6)
1427		if b.Parent != "" {
1428			metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("parent=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(b.Parent)))
1429		}
1430		if specID, _ := b.Extra["spec-id"].(string); strings.TrimSpace(specID) != "" {
1431			metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("spec-id=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(strings.TrimSpace(specID))))
1432		}
1433		metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("base-rev=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(baseRev)))
1434		metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("bundle=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(artifacts.DirRel)))
1435		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <metadata %s/>\n", strings.Join(metaAttrs, " "))
1436		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n")
1437	
1438		// For minimal budget, omit full governing refs and only include bead metadata.
1439		// This significantly reduces prompt size for cheap-tier attempts on local models.
1440		if contextBudget == "minimal" {
1441			sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n    <note>No governing references.</note>\n  </governing>\n")
1442		} else {
1443			if len(refs) == 0 {
1444				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <governing>\n    <note>%s</note>\n  </governing>\n", xmlEscape(executeBeadMissingGoverningText))
1445			} else {
1446				sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
1447				for _, ref := range refs {
1448					attrs := fmt.Sprintf("id=\"%s\" path=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(ref.ID), xmlAttrEscape(ref.Path))
1449					if strings.TrimSpace(ref.Title) == "" {
1450						fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref %s/>\n", attrs)
1451					} else {
1452						fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref %s>%s</ref>\n", attrs, xmlEscape(strings.TrimSpace(ref.Title)))
1453					}
1454				}
1455				sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n")
1456			}
1457		}
1458	
1459		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(executeBeadInstructionsText(harness), "{{.AttemptDir}}", artifacts.DirRel)
1460		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", xmlEscape(instructions))
1461	
1462		sb.WriteString("</execute-bead>\n")
1463	
1464		return []byte(sb.String()), "synthesized", nil
1465	}

1471		sum := sha256.Sum256(promptContent)
1472		return hex.EncodeToString(sum[:])
1473	}

17	// representativePromptBead returns the deterministic bead used by the
18	// prompt-size fixture and the prompt_sha test. Holding it constant means
19	// rendered prompt sha and word/byte counts only change when the static
20	// instructions change — which is exactly what we want to surface.
21	func representativePromptBead() *bead.Bead {
22		return &bead.Bead{
23			ID:    "ddx-promptsize-fixture",
24			Title: "Representative bead for prompt-size measurement",
25			Description: "Static fixture used by Story 12 prompt-size report and " +
26				"prompt_sha analytics. Description body is deliberately ordinary " +
27				"prose so word/byte counts reflect the static instructions, not " +
28				"the bead body.",
29			Acceptance: "AC1: prompt renders deterministically.\n" +
30				"AC2: word and byte counts are reported per (variant, contextBudget).",
31			Labels: []string{"phase:2", "story:12", "tier:cheap"},
32			Extra:  map[string]any{"spec-id": "FEAT-022"},
33		}
34	}
35	
36	// renderPromptForSize renders a prompt for the given harness selector and
37	// contextBudget using a fixed in-memory artifact bundle. It does not touch
38	// the filesystem so the fixture is hermetic.
39	func renderPromptForSize(t *testing.T, harness, contextBudget string) []byte {
40		t.Helper()
41		const attemptID = "20260101T000000-promptsize"
42		arts := &executeBeadArtifacts{
43			DirRel:      ".ddx/executions/" + attemptID,
44			PromptRel:   ".ddx/executions/" + attemptID + "/prompt.md",
45			ManifestRel: ".ddx/executions/" + attemptID + "/manifest.json",
46			ResultRel:   ".ddx/executions/" + attemptID + "/result.json",
47			ChecksRel:   ".ddx/executions/" + attemptID + "/checks.json",
48			UsageRel:    ".ddx/executions/" + attemptID + "/usage.json",
49		}
50		b := representativePromptBead()
51		// Empty refs to keep the fixture hermetic — buildPrompt will emit the
52		// missing-governing fallback note for the non-minimal budget, which
53		// matches the AC for that case.
54		content, _, err := buildPrompt(t.TempDir(), b, nil, arts, "deadbeefdeadbeef", "", harness, contextBudget)
55		if err != nil {
56			t.Fatalf("buildPrompt(%s, %q): %v", harness, contextBudget, err)
57		}
58		return content
59	}

61	// TestPromptSizeReport renders the execute-bead prompt for both variants
62	// (Claude harness, Agent harness) at each contextBudget label ("" full,
63	// "minimal") and writes a deterministic word/byte report. CI uploads the
64	// report path named by DDX_PROMPT_SIZE_REPORT as an artifact so accidental
65	// prompt bloat shows up as a diff in the artifact across runs.
66	func TestPromptSizeReport(t *testing.T) {
67		type row struct {
68			Variant       string `json:"variant"`
69			Harness       string `json:"harness"`
70			ContextBudget string `json:"context_budget"`
71			Words         int    `json:"words"`
72			Bytes         int    `json:"bytes"`
73			PromptSHA     string `json:"prompt_sha"`
74		}
75	
76		// Selector at execute_bead.go routes (agent|fiz|embedded) to the Agent
77		// variant; everything else (claude/codex/opencode/unknown) to the Claude
78		// variant. One representative harness per variant is enough.
79		cases := []struct{ variant, harness string }{
80			{"claude", "claude"},
81			{"agent", "agent"},
82		}
83		budgets := []string{"", "minimal"}
84	
85		rows := make([]row, 0, len(cases)*len(budgets))
86		for _, c := range cases {
87			for _, budget := range budgets {
88				content := renderPromptForSize(t, c.harness, budget)
89				sum := sha256.Sum256(content)
90				rows = append(rows, row{
91					Variant:       c.variant,
92					Harness:       c.harness,
93					ContextBudget: budget,
94					Words:         len(strings.Fields(string(content))),
95					Bytes:         len(content),
96					PromptSHA:     hex.EncodeToString(sum[:]),
97				})
98			}
99		}
100	
101		sort.Slice(rows, func(i, j int) bool {
102			if rows[i].Variant != rows[j].Variant {
103				return rows[i].Variant < rows[j].Variant
104			}
105			return rows[i].ContextBudget < rows[j].ContextBudget
106		})
107	
108		var report strings.Builder
109		report.WriteString("# execute-bead prompt size report\n")
110		report.WriteString("# variant\tharness\tcontext_budget\twords\tbytes\tprompt_sha\n")
111		for _, r := range rows {
112			budget := r.ContextBudget
113			if budget == "" {
114				budget = "full"
115			}
116			fmt.Fprintf(&report, "%s\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%d\t%s\n",
117				r.Variant, r.Harness, budget, r.Words, r.Bytes, r.PromptSHA)
118		}
119	
120		// Always log the report to test output — that alone makes CI surface
121		// it via the existing test-log capture even if the artifact step is
122		// not configured.
123		t.Logf("\n%s", report.String())
124	
125		// Sanity: every row produced non-empty output.
126		for _, r := range rows {
127			if r.Words == 0 || r.Bytes == 0 {
128				t.Errorf("empty rendered prompt for variant=%s budget=%q", r.Variant, r.ContextBudget)
129			}
130		}
131		// Minimal must be strictly smaller than full for both variants — the
132		// fixture's reason for existing is to catch regressions on the cheap-tier
133		// path.
134		byKey := map[string]row{}
135		for _, r := range rows {
136			byKey[r.Variant+"|"+r.ContextBudget] = r
137		}
138		for _, v := range []string{"claude", "agent"} {
139			full := byKey[v+"|"]
140			min := byKey[v+"|minimal"]
141			if min.Bytes >= full.Bytes {
142				t.Errorf("variant %s: minimal bytes %d not smaller than full bytes %d",
143					v, min.Bytes, full.Bytes)
144			}
145		}
146	
147		// Write the human-readable report alongside a JSON sibling for
148		// machine consumers. CI sets DDX_PROMPT_SIZE_REPORT to a path under the
149		// workspace and uploads it as an artifact; locally the test falls back
150		// to t.TempDir() so it never pollutes the working tree.
151		outPath := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv("DDX_PROMPT_SIZE_REPORT"))
152		if outPath == "" {
153			outPath = filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "prompt-size-report.txt")
154		}
155		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(outPath), 0o755); err != nil {
156			t.Fatalf("mkdir report dir: %v", err)
157		}
158		if err := os.WriteFile(outPath, []byte(report.String()), 0o644); err != nil {
159			t.Fatalf("writing prompt-size report: %v", err)
160		}
161		jsonPath := strings.TrimSuffix(outPath, filepath.Ext(outPath)) + ".json"
162		jb, err := json.MarshalIndent(rows, "", "  ")
163		if err != nil {
164			t.Fatalf("marshal rows: %v", err)
165		}
166		if err := os.WriteFile(jsonPath, append(jb, '\n'), 0o644); err != nil {
167			t.Fatalf("writing prompt-size report json: %v", err)
168		}
169		t.Logf("prompt-size report written to %s (and %s)", outPath, jsonPath)
170	}
171	
172	// TestPromptSHA_DeterministicAcrossRenders asserts that the helper
173	// promptSHA used to populate manifest.prompt_sha is stable across renders
174	// of the same inputs. Without this, analytics grouping by prompt_sha would
175	// fragment across attempts that share a prompt.
176	func TestPromptSHA_DeterministicAcrossRenders(t *testing.T) {
177		a := renderPromptForSize(t, "claude", "")
178		b := renderPromptForSize(t, "claude", "")
179		if promptSHA(a) != promptSHA(b) {
180			t.Fatalf("promptSHA not deterministic: %s vs %s", promptSHA(a), promptSHA(b))
181		}
182		c := renderPromptForSize(t, "agent", "")
183		if promptSHA(a) == promptSHA(c) {
184			t.Fatalf("promptSHA collided across variants — selector is not influencing the prompt body")
185		}
186	}

66			{"unknown_error", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
67				"something exploded", FailureModeUnknown},
68			{"no_error_text_non_zero", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 137, "",
69				FailureModeUnknown},
70	
71			// Ordering: timeout wins over test/build keywords when both appear.
72			{"timeout_beats_test_failure", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
73				"test timed out after 5m", FailureModeTimeout},
74			// Ordering: auth wins over generic keywords.
75			{"auth_beats_build", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed, 1,
76				"401 unauthorized: build failed", FailureModeAuthError},
77		}
78	
79		for _, tc := range cases {
80			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
81				got := ClassifyFailureMode(tc.outcome, tc.exitCode, tc.errMsg)
82				if got != tc.want {
83					t.Fatalf("ClassifyFailureMode(%q, %d, %q) = %q, want %q",
84						tc.outcome, tc.exitCode, tc.errMsg, got, tc.want)
85				}
86			})
87		}
88	}

90	// TestFailureModeLandingRefines exercises classifyLandingFailureMode, which
91	// is how ApplyLandingToResult folds landing-level signals (merge conflict,
92	// gate failure) into the final failure_mode. A clean merge clears the
93	// worker's mode; merge conflict and gate failure override it; other
94	// preserved reasons defer to the worker's classification.
95	func TestFailureModeLandingRefines(t *testing.T) {
96		gateFailTest := []GateCheckResult{{
97			DefinitionID: "go-test",
98			Required:     true,
99			Status:       "fail",
100			Stderr:       "--- FAIL: TestX",
101		}}
102		gateFailBuild := []GateCheckResult{{
103			DefinitionID: "go-build",
104			Required:     true,
105			Status:       "fail",
106			Stderr:       "undefined: foo.Bar",
107		}}
108	
109		cases := []struct {
110			name       string
111			outcome    string
112			reason     string
113			gates      []GateCheckResult
114			workerMode string
115			want       string
116		}{
117			{"merged_clears_worker_mode", "merged", "", nil, FailureModeUnknown, ""},
118			{"no_changes_outcome", "no-changes", "agent made no commits", nil, "", FailureModeNoChanges},
119			{"no_evidence_outcome", "no-evidence", "agent exited without a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt", nil, "", FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced},
120	
121			{"preserved_merge_conflict", "preserved", "merge conflict", nil, "", FailureModeMergeConflict},
122			{"preserved_ff_not_possible", "preserved", "ff-merge not possible", nil, "", FailureModeMergeConflict},
123			{"preserved_merge_failed", "preserved", "merge failed", nil, "", FailureModeMergeConflict},
124	
125			{"preserved_post_run_build", "preserved", "post-run checks failed", gateFailBuild, "", FailureModeBuildFailure},
126			{"preserved_post_run_test", "preserved", "post-run checks failed", gateFailTest, "", FailureModeTestFailure},
127			{"preserved_post_run_empty_defaults_to_test", "preserved", "post-run checks failed", nil, "", FailureModeTestFailure},
128	
129			{"preserved_agent_execution_failed_keeps_worker", "preserved", "agent execution failed", nil, FailureModeContextOverflow, FailureModeContextOverflow},
130			{"preserved_no_merge_keeps_worker", "preserved", "--no-merge specified", nil, FailureModeTimeout, FailureModeTimeout},
131			{"preserved_unknown_reason_falls_back_to_unknown", "preserved", "something else", nil, "", FailureModeUnknown},
132	
133			{"error_keeps_worker_mode", "error", "agent execution failed", nil, FailureModeAuthError, FailureModeAuthError},
134			{"error_unknown_when_worker_empty", "error", "agent execution failed", nil, "", FailureModeUnknown},
135		}
136	
137		for _, tc := range cases {
138			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
139				got := classifyLandingFailureMode(tc.outcome, tc.reason, tc.gates, tc.workerMode)
140				if got != tc.want {
141					t.Fatalf("classifyLandingFailureMode(%q, %q, %d-gates, %q) = %q, want %q",
142						tc.outcome, tc.reason, len(tc.gates), tc.workerMode, got, tc.want)
143				}
144			})
145		}
146	}

148	// TestFailureModeApplyLandingToResult verifies that ApplyLandingToResult
149	// writes the refined failure_mode onto the result, giving callers a single
150	// unified record. This is the contract callers of result.json depend on.
151	func TestFailureModeApplyLandingToResult(t *testing.T) {
152		// Worker recorded a context overflow; landing then merged the result
153		// cleanly (unlikely but possible if the worker bounced mid-run and a
154		// retry landed). The landing decision wins: failure_mode clears.
155		res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
156			BeadID:      "ddx-test",
157			BaseRev:     "a",
158			ResultRev:   "b",
159			ExitCode:    0,
160			Outcome:     ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded,
161			FailureMode: FailureModeContextOverflow, // carried from a prior attempt
162		}

164		if res.FailureMode != "" {
165			t.Fatalf("merged landing should clear FailureMode, got %q", res.FailureMode)
166		}
167	
168		// Worker succeeded; landing could not merge. FailureMode becomes
169		// merge_conflict so measurement can bucket the failure.

172			BaseRev:     "a",
173			ResultRev:   "b",
174			ExitCode:    0,
175			Outcome:     ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded,
176			FailureMode: "",
177		}
178		ApplyLandingToResult(res, &BeadLandingResult{Outcome: "preserved", Reason: "merge conflict"})
179		if res.FailureMode != FailureModeMergeConflict {
180			t.Fatalf("merge conflict landing should set merge_conflict, got %q", res.FailureMode)
181		}
182	
183		// Worker succeeded; gate failed with a build diagnostic in stderr.
184		res = &ExecuteBeadResult{
185			BeadID:    "ddx-test",
186			BaseRev:   "a",
187			ResultRev: "b",
188			ExitCode:  0,
189			Outcome:   ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded,
190		}
191		ApplyLandingToResult(res, &BeadLandingResult{
192			Outcome: "preserved",
193			Reason:  "post-run checks failed",
194			GateResults: []GateCheckResult{{
195				DefinitionID: "go-build",
196				Required:     true,
197				Status:       "fail",
198				Stderr:       "undefined: foo.Bar",
199			}},
200		})
201		if res.FailureMode != FailureModeBuildFailure {
202			t.Fatalf("build-diagnostic gate failure should set build_failure, got %q", res.FailureMode)
203		}
204	
205		// No-changes landing outcome always maps to no_changes regardless of
206		// what the worker recorded.
207		res = &ExecuteBeadResult{
208			BeadID:      "ddx-test",
209			BaseRev:     "a",
210			ResultRev:   "a",
211			ExitCode:    0,
212			Outcome:     ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges,
213			FailureMode: FailureModeNoChanges,
214		}
215		ApplyLandingToResult(res, &BeadLandingResult{Outcome: "no-changes", Reason: "agent made no commits"})
216		if res.FailureMode != FailureModeNoChanges {
217			t.Fatalf("no-changes landing should set no_changes, got %q", res.FailureMode)
218		}
219	
220		res = &ExecuteBeadResult{
221			BeadID:      "ddx-test",
222			BaseRev:     "a",
223			ResultRev:   "a",
224			ExitCode:    0,
225			Outcome:     ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence,
226			FailureMode: FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced,
227		}

// Shared instruction-block constants. Each holds a section opener emitted
// verbatim by both execute-bead variants; per-variant prose continues
// inline after these constants. Extracting the common substrings keeps
// each guardrail (Step 0 size check, investigation/report path, review
// gate, bead-override clause, core constraints) in a single source
// location while preserving the exact wording each variant ships today.
const instrStep0SizeCheck = "\n\n## Step 0: size check (do this first)\n\nBefore writing any code, decide whether this bead "

const instrInvestigationReports = "\n\n## Investigation and report outputs\n\nIf the bead asks you to \"output a report\", \"document findings\", or otherwise produce a freestanding artifact that is not source code, write it under `{{.AttemptDir}}/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — paths outside the repo are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and do not survive between machines"

const instrReviewGate = "\n\n## Quality bar and the review step\n\nAfter your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass"

const instrBeadOverride = "\n\n## The bead contract overrides project defaults\n\nThe bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. "

const instrCoreConstraints = "\n\n## Constraints\n\n- Work only inside this execution worktree.\n- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.\n- **Never run `ddx init`** — "

// executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText is the per-bead task instructions emitted
// inside <instructions> when running against a harness that carries its own
// rich system prompt (claude, codex, opencode). These harnesses already know
// how to read files, run tools, and manage sessions — this text adds only the
// DDx-specific execution contract.
const executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText = `You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.` +
	instrStep0SizeCheck +
	`is sized for a single bounded-context attempt. Coarse signals that the bead is too big:

- More than ~6 acceptance criteria spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables rather than a single coherent change.
- Description explicitly lists multiple feature-sized pieces ("AC4 is a tier-inference engine", "AC5 introduces a new status type").
- You estimate more than ~500 lines of meaningful code change across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If the bead is too big, do NOT attempt the work. Instead, in this single attempt:

1. Use the ` + "`bash`" + ` tool to run ` + "`ddx bead create`" + ` to file child beads, one per coherent slice of work. Pass each child a focused title, description, and acceptance criteria; copy labels and ` + "`spec-id`" + ` from the parent.
2. Use ` + "`ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`" + ` to record the dependency edge.
3. Append a short note to the parent (` + "`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`" + `) so future runs see why no implementation landed.
4. Write ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` explaining the decomposition: list each child bead ID and the slice it covers, and stop.

A clean decomposition that produces tractable children is a successful attempt. Do not push partial implementation alongside decomposition.

If the bead IS sized for one attempt, continue with the rest of these instructions.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically ` + "`cargo test`" + ` and ` + "`cargo clippy`" + ` for Rust, ` + "`bun test`" + ` or ` + "`npm test`" + ` for JS/TS, ` + "`go test`" + ` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with ` + "`git add <specific-paths>`" + ` — never ` + "`git add -A`" + `, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with ` + "`[<bead-id>]`" + `. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, writing ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` is mandatory before you exit. Include: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. If you exit without a commit and without this file, DDx records ` + "`no_evidence_produced`" + ` and treats the attempt as a harness failure, not as legitimate no_changes. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.` +
	instrInvestigationReports +
	`, which causes the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/<short-name>.md`" + `. Stage and commit the report alongside any code changes so it lands in the merge.` +
	instrBeadOverride +
	`If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.` +
	instrReviewGate +
	` so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a ` + "`<review-findings>`" + ` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.` +
	instrCoreConstraints +
	`the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.`

// executeBeadInstructionsAgentText is the per-bead task instructions emitted
// inside <instructions> when running against the embedded Fizeau harness,
// which has a minimal system prompt and needs more scaffolding to produce
// reliable output. This version is more explicit about tools, process, and
// stopping cleanly after the commit (to avoid the known post-commit runaway
// failure mode).
const executeBeadInstructionsAgentText = `You are a coding agent executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. You have tools: read, write, edit, bash, ls, grep, find. Use them directly — do not shell out to cat/tail/rg/find; use the tools.

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract. Every AC checkbox must be satisfied by code you write in this pass.` +
	instrStep0SizeCheck +
	`fits in one bounded-context attempt. Coarse signals it does not:

- More than ~6 acceptance criteria spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description explicitly names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- You estimate more than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, do NOT attempt the work. Instead, in this single attempt:

1. Run ` + "`ddx bead create`" + ` (via bash) to file child beads, one per coherent slice — focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id.
2. Run ` + "`ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`" + ` to wire the dependency edge.
3. Run ` + "`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`" + ` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` listing each child bead ID and the slice it covers, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

If sized for one attempt, continue.

## Process

1. **Read first.** Before writing any code, read the files the bead description names. If the description says "A1 landed X at <path>", read that path. If it references a spec section, read that section. Do NOT start editing without seeing the existing code — this is how you avoid making the same change twice or breaking an invariant you did not know about.

2. **Plan the work briefly.** Internally note which files you will touch, which tests you will add, and which verification commands you will run. Keep the plan short — one or two sentences.

3. **Implement.** Use ` + "`edit`" + ` for targeted changes to existing files and ` + "`write`" + ` for brand-new files. Use ` + "`read`" + ` (not ` + "`bash: cat`" + `) to read files. Use ` + "`grep`" + ` (not ` + "`bash: rg`" + `) to search. Use ` + "`ls`" + ` (not ` + "`bash: ls`" + `) for directory listings.

4. **Verify before committing.** Run the project's test and lint commands — typically ` + "`cargo test -p <crate>`" + ` and ` + "`cargo clippy -p <crate> -- -D warnings`" + ` for Rust, ` + "`bun test`" + ` for JS/TS, ` + "`go test`" + ` for Go. The bead description will usually name the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If anything fails, diagnose and fix it before committing.

5. **Commit exactly once.** Use ` + "`git add <specific-paths>`" + ` with the file paths you actually changed — never ` + "`git add -A`" + `, which can pick up unrelated files in the worktree. Use a conventional-commit subject ending with ` + "`[<bead-id>]`" + `. Commit implementation and tests in the same commit so the reviewer sees them together.

6. **Stop after the commit succeeds.** Return immediately. Do not continue reading files, running extra tests, or asking yourself follow-up questions — the work is done, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success to the orchestrator. Continuing past the commit risks runaway loops.

## If you cannot finish

Writing ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` is mandatory before you exit without a commit. Include: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up would need. If you exit without a commit and without this file, DDx records ` + "`no_evidence_produced`" + ` and treats the attempt as a harness failure, not as legitimate no_changes. Do NOT commit partial or red code — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt.` +
	instrInvestigationReports +
	`. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use that; otherwise default to ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/<short-name>.md`" + `. Stage and commit the report alongside any code changes.` +
	instrReviewGate +
	`.**

If this prompt already contains a ` + "`<review-findings>`" + ` section, every BLOCKING finding in it is a concrete thing the previous attempt missed. Address each one before declaring the work complete — do not declare no_changes with blocking findings still unaddressed.` +
	instrBeadOverride +
	`If the bead asks for new files, write them. Conservative rules (YAGNI, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.` +
	instrCoreConstraints +
	`it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not touch CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.
- Stage only the files you intentionally changed.`
